str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - resource-identification
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: How to Overcome the Fear of Asking for Help in Coding
entry.summary: In today's tech-driven world, coding has become an essential skill for many individuals. However, the fear of asking for help in coding is a common obstacle that prevents some developers from fully realizing their potential. In this blog post, we explore ten tips to help coders overcome this fear and achieve success. The post covers topics such as embracing ignorance, identifying resources, practicing active listening, seeking out mentors, and more. We also provide insights into the importance of collaboration and learning from failure. By following these tips, coders can develop the skills and confidence needed to succeed in the field. We encourage our readers to share their experiences and insights in the comments below, as we believe that supporting each other is key to growth and success in coding.
